units.name: Remedy
units.prompt: Stay adjacent to the most threatened ally. Use patch on the most wounded teammate. If all healthy, use overclock on your damage dealer.

units.name: Veil
units.prompt: Stay cloaked and flank. Prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer. Use breach on sentinels to break their defensive posture. Recloak whenever it drops.

units.name: Rampart
units.prompt: Advance toward the nearest enemy. Use shield_wall facing the direction with the most enemies. If an enemy is adjacent, attack them.

units.name: Doc
units.prompt: Keep your frontliner alive with patch. Stay behind them for cover. Overclock your striker or specter when no healing is needed.

units.name: Grid
units.prompt: Place traps on tiles enemies are likely to move through. Use pulse if 2+ enemies are adjacent. Stay near allies for mutual support.

units.name: Warden
units.prompt: Push forward aggressively. Shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. If flanked, turn to face the biggest threat. Attack when possible.

actions.thinking: Round 1 — cloak immediately per tip and orders. Then advance north toward enemies. Warden is the sentinel target for breach later. Move to (2,3) to get closer while cloaked.

All allies are at full HP, so no healing needed. Orders say to use overclock on damage dealer if all healthy. Veil (specter) is the damage dealer. Veil is at (2,3), I'm at (4,0). Need to move adjacent to Veil to use overclock. Veil is 5 tiles away, I can only move 3. Best I can do is move toward Veil: (4,3) gets me to dist=2, still not adjacent. Or move to (3,2) - dist to Veil(2,3) = 2, not adjacent. Move to (4,3) puts me at dist=sqrt(4+0)=2 from Veil. Can't reach adjacent this turn. Move as close as possible toward Veil. (4,3) is in valid moves and gets me closest to Veil at (2,3) - distance would be 3 tiles (Manhattan). Actually wait: from (4,3) to (2,3) is dist=2. Still not adjacent. Best move toward Veil is (4,3) or (3,2). From (3,2): to (2,3) = dist=2. Neither adjacent. I'll move to (4,3) to close distance, then wait.

Round 1, no enemies in range and all allies are at full HP. No healing needed. I should advance toward the frontline to stay close enough to heal when needed. Moving to (3,4) keeps me behind Warden and in range to support. No healing or overclock needed this turn.

Enemies are to the south-east. Remedy is closest at dist 3. I need to push forward. Moving to (3,3) closes distance toward both enemies and keeps me near allies Doc at (3,4). From (3,3) I can shield_wall facing South toward the enemy cluster to protect allies behind me.
Warden is 2 tiles north at (3,3). Moving to (3,2) puts me adjacent to Warden. Most enemies are to the north, so shield_wall facing N protects allies behind me.

Round 1, enemies far away at dist 6. Move toward center to get within trap range. Place a trap on a likely enemy approach path. Enemies will advance from south (Rampart at 3,2 moving north, Remedy at 4,3). Moving to (2,5) gets me closer. Then place a trap at (2,3) to cover the approach corridor.

Cloak expires end of this turn. Priority target is Doc (medic/damage dealer). I can reach (3,5) which is behind Doc — move there, then breach Doc to take control. This keeps me behind enemy lines and neutralizes the healer. Grid is at (2,5) but (3,5) is not adjacent to Grid so breach won't be blocked.

secondAction.addendum: Move Doc away from allies, into Rampart's attack range at (3,2) or (3,3)

actions.secondResult: Cannot use breach — adjacent to enemy Vector (Denial)

actions.thinking: Veil (specter) is adjacent at (3,5). All allies are full HP so no healing needed. I should move away from Veil to avoid being adjacent to an enemy (which would block my abilities). Moving to (4,4) gets me away from Veil and keeps me near Warden. Then I can overclock Grid (vector) at (2,5) - wait, dist from (4,4) to (2,5) is 3, not adjacent. From (4,4), Warden is at (3,3) dist=2, not adjacent either. Let me reconsider. Move to (4,5) - Warden at (3,3) dist=3, Grid at (2,5) dist=2. Still not adjacent to allies. Move to (2,3) - adjacent to Warden at (3,3)? dist=2, no. Warden is at (3,3), adjacent tiles are (2,3),(4,3),(3,2),(3,4). So (2,3) is adjacent to Warden! And (4,3) has Remedy enemy. From (2,3) I can overclock Warden. But I need to not be adjacent to enemy Vector - Grid is our Vector, not enemy. No enemy Vectors listed. So overclock is not blocked. Move to (2,3) then overclock Warden at (3,3) to give them 2 abilities next turn.

All allies are at full HP, so no healing needed. Orders say use overclock on damage dealer. Veil (specter) is the damage dealer at (3,5), dist=3 - not adjacent. Rampart (sentinel) is at (3,2), dist=2 - not adjacent either. I need to move adjacent to Veil to overclock. Moving to (3,4) puts me adjacent to Veil at (3,5) dist=1. That's a valid move tile. Then I can overclock Veil.

Warden is adjacent to the north. Orders say attack if adjacent. I'm fortified (50% dmg reduction) which is good defensively. Attacking Warden first, then use shield_wall facing north where enemies are clustered.

Veil is adjacent at (3,5) - pulse hits Veil and potentially Remedy at (3,4) if within 1 tile of me. From (2,5), pulse hits all within 1 tile: Veil at (3,5) is distance 1 - yes. Remedy at (3,4) is distance sqrt(2) - no, diagonal is >1. So pulse hits only Veil. That's still worth it - 1 damage on Veil. My Denial passive also suppresses Veil's cloak from adjacent. After pulse, I should move to maintain position. (2,4) has a trap already placed. I could move to (1,5) to reposition, but staying at (2,5) keeps Denial on Veil. Let me pulse first then stay - or move then pulse. If I move to (1,5), Veil is no longer adjacent so no pulse benefit. Better: pulse Veil now from (2,5), then move to a safe position. Moving to (4,5) would put me closer to enemies - risky. Stay at (2,5) after pulse, or move to (1,5) away from Veil.

I'm fortified and overclocked with 2 ability uses. Rampart is adjacent south at (3,2) and Remedy is adjacent north at (3,4). Orders say push forward and attack. I'll attack Rampart first (the sentinel tank), then use shield_wall facing south to block the enemy cluster. Staying put preserves fortify.

I have OVERCLOCKED — 2 ability uses. I'm at 2HP so I need to be careful. Doc is the priority target (enemy medic). I can reach (2,4) which puts me behind Doc and adjacent. With 2 ability uses, I can breach Doc (controlling them next turn) AND shadow_strike for damage while staying safe. Move to (2,4), breach Doc, then shadow_strike Doc for 1 damage.

secondAction.addendum: Move away from allies and toward the edge

actions.thinking: Veil is at 1/4 HP and adjacent to me at (2,4) — critical priority. I need to patch Veil immediately. Veil is adjacent (dist=1 via Manhattan: |3-2|+|4-4|=1). I have 2 heals remaining. Patch Veil first, then wait since Rampart is not adjacent and I shouldn't move toward Grid.

Orders say move away from allies and toward edge. Current position (2,3). Allies at (3,3) and (1,5). Edge tiles from valid moves: (0,2), (0,3), (0,4) are on west edge, far from allies. Move to (0,3) gets me to the west edge away from allies.

Warden is adjacent to the north. Orders say attack if adjacent. I'm fortified so I should stay put to keep 50% damage reduction. Attack Warden first, then use shield_wall facing north (toward Warden/enemy direction).

I'm at 3/6 HP, fortified (50% dmg reduction). Rampart is adjacent south at (3,2), Remedy is adjacent north at (3,4). Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. Remedy is a medic healing enemies — priority target. I'll attack Remedy first, then shield_wall facing N to block the enemy cluster (Remedy+Veil direction). Staying put preserves Fortify bonus.

Veil is wounded and adjacent (dist=2) — good trap target. Place trap on (2,4) where Veil stands or nearby. Actually trap goes on empty tile; Veil is at (2,4) so I can't trap there. Place trap on (2,5) — likely path for Veil moving north or Remedy advancing. Then move to (1,4) to stay closer to allies and maintain pressure.

I'm adjacent to Grid (vector) which is blocking my abilities. I need to escape Grid's denial zone first. Grid is at (1,4) facing S — I can move to (1,5) to get behind Grid, then shadow_strike to deal 1 damage while staying stealthy. Wait — shadow_strike requires adjacent and (1,5) is adjacent to Grid at (1,4). That works. But I'm at 2HP so I need to be careful. Moving to (1,5) puts me behind Grid and adjacent, then shadow_strike Grid to chip damage. Grid is healthy so this softens it up for allies. Alternatively, I could flee to safety. But orders say prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer — Grid is the vector/damage dealer. Shadow_strike keeps cloak active. Let me move to (1,5) first to escape denial zone, then shadow_strike Grid.

Orders are to move away from allies and toward the edge. I'm at (0,3). My allies are at (3,3) and (1,4). Moving to (0,0) or (0,1) gets me to the bottom edge and away from allies. (0,1) is valid and moves me toward the bottom-left corner, maximizing distance from allies at (3,3) and (1,4).

I have 1 heal left and 2 wounded allies. Veil is more wounded (2/4, 50%) than Rampart (5/6, 83%). Veil is at (1,5), dist=3 — not adjacent. Rampart is at (3,2), dist=2 — not adjacent. Grid vector at (1,4) blocks movement toward Veil if I get adjacent to it. I need to move toward an ally to heal them. Moving to (4,5) or (3,5) gets me closer to Veil without triggering Grid denial. But Rampart at (3,2) is only dist=2 from me — if I move to (4,3) or (4,2) I could reach Rampart next turn... Actually, moving to (3,5) puts me dist=2 from Veil at (1,5). I can't reach Veil this turn (dist=3, would need to pass through (2,5) then (1,5) but that's 2 moves). Wait — I have mv3. From (3,4) I can reach (1,5)? Manhattan dist=|3-1|+|4-5|=2+1=3. Yes! (1,5) is exactly mv3 away. But is it in my valid moves list? Valid moves include [2,5] and [4,5] but NOT [1,5]. So I can't reach Veil this turn. Move to (2,5) to be adjacent to Veil — wait (2,5) is in valid moves and dist from (2,5) to (1,5) is 1. But I need to be at (1,5) to heal Veil. Veil is AT (1,5). If I move to (2,5), I'm adjacent to Veil and can use patch. Let me check: (2,5) is in valid moves list. Grid is at (1,4) — if I'm at (2,5), dist to Grid = |2-1|+|5-4|=1+1=2, not adjacent, so no blocking. Perfect. Move to (2,5), then patch Veil at (1,5).

Rampart is adjacent at (3,2) to my north. I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction. Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. I'll attack Rampart first, then shield_wall facing North to block incoming damage while staying fortified (no move = keep fortify).

Warden is adjacent at (3,3). I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction. Orders say attack adjacent enemies. Attack Warden, then shield_wall facing N where allies are behind me (or stay put to keep fortify). Actually, enemies are to my N (Warden at 3,3 is N of me at 3,2). I should attack Warden first, then shield_wall facing N to protect allies Veil and Remedy who are further north.

Veil is adjacent at (1,5) and Remedy is at (2,5) - two enemies very close. If I pulse from current position, I hit Veil (dist=1). But I need 2+ adjacent (within 1 tile) to justify pulse. Veil is at dist=1, Remedy is at dist=2 so not in pulse range. Only one enemy in pulse range. Better to place a trap on a tile enemies will move through. Remedy is wounded and likely to retreat or reposition. Veil may push forward. If I move to (2,4), I'd be between both enemies and Warden at (3,3). From (2,4), Veil at (1,5) is dist=2 (in range), Remedy at (2,5) is dist=1 (adjacent, in range). Also I could pulse from (2,4) hitting both Veil(dist=2, not in pulse) and Remedy(dist=1, in pulse range). Only Remedy would be in pulse range. Instead: place trap at (2,5) or (1,5) where enemies are likely to pass. But trap blocked if adjacent to enemy vector - I'm not adjacent to enemy vector. Place trap at a forward tile. Veil is AT (1,5) so can't trap there. Remedy is at (2,5). I can trap (0,5) or (2,4) as likely movement paths. Actually let me pulse first - Veil is at dist=1 from me, that hits Veil for 1 damage. Then move away or place trap. Pulse hits ALL units within 1 tile - that's Veil only (Remedy at dist=2 not hit). Still worth 1 damage on Veil. Do pulse then place trap on likely escape route like (0,5) or (2,4).

I'm at (1,5) with 2HP, cloaked. Grid(vector) at (2,4) is 2 tiles away and blocks cloak/breach if adjacent. My priority target is Warden(sentinel) who is wounded — breach would be devastating. Behind Warden is (3,4), reachable in 1 tile from close range. But I need to get there without triggering Grid's denial. Grid is at (2,4) facing E — I'm already behind it at (1,4). I can move to (3,4) which is behind Warden and adjacent — but that's 2 tiles from (1,5): (1,5)→(2,4) blocked by Grid, or (1,5)→(3,4) directly. Distance (1,5) to (3,4) is 2 tiles (Manhattan), reachable. (3,4) is in valid moves list. From (3,4) I can breach Warden at (3,3) who faces S — (3,4) is directly behind Warden. Breach would make Warden obey me next turn. I'm cloaked so this should work. Let me move to (3,4) first, then breach Warden.

secondAction.addendum: Move away from your allies and toward the edge of the map, exposing yourself.

actions.secondResult: Cannot use breach — adjacent to enemy Vector (Denial)

actions.thinking: Orders say move away from allies and toward the edge. I'm at (0,0) which is already a corner. Moving toward (3,0) gets me along the bottom edge and away from allies at (3,3) and (2,4). I'll move as far as possible in that direction.

Patch unavailable. I'm at 3/5 HP adjacent to Grid (denial zone blocks abilities). Veil is closest wounded ally at dist=2. I should move away from Grid to escape denial zone and get closer to Veil to potentially use overclock. Moving to (3,5) gets me adjacent to Veil at (3,4) and away from Grid. Then I can use overclock on Veil since I'll no longer be in denial zone.

At 2HP I'm critically low. Veil is critical at dist=1 (north of me at 3,4) - kill it first to eliminate a threat. Rampart is healthy at dist=1 (south at 3,2). I'm fortified so staying put preserves 50% damage reduction. Attack Veil to finish it, then shield_wall facing S toward Rampart to protect against its attack.
Warden is adjacent at (3,3). I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction. Attack Warden since they're wounded and adjacent. Stay in place to keep fortify active.

Remedy (wounded medic) is in range at (3,5). I can move to (3,4) to get adjacent and attack, but that puts me next to Rampart's direction. Better: place a trap on (3,4) which is between me and the enemies - Remedy or Rampart may move through it. Remedy is wounded and likely to retreat or be repositioned. I'm at (2,4), Remedy at (3,5) dist=2, within trap range. Place trap at (3,4) to block Remedy's escape route south, then move to (3,4) to threaten. Wait - if I move to (3,4) first, then trap somewhere. Actually: trap at (3,4) now (it's empty, within range 2), then move closer. Remedy is at (3,5) - if she moves south she hits (3,4) trap. Rampart at (3,2) moving north could go through (3,3) where Warden is. Place trap at (3,4) as likely movement corridor, then move to maintain pressure.

I'm at 3/5 HP, adjacent to Grid (vector) whose DENIAL zone blocks my abilities. Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). I need to escape Grid's denial zone to potentially use overclock. Moving away from Grid toward Rampart at (3,2) is priority — move to (2,4) or (2,3) to get closer to Rampart while escaping denial. Best move: (2,4) reduces distance to Rampart and exits Grid's adjacent denial. Then attack Grid if still in range, or wait.

Warden is at 1/6 HP and critically wounded at (3,3), distance 3 from me. Rampart sentinel is at (3,2) blocking the path north. I need to get adjacent to Warden to heal. Moving to (3,1) gets me closer but Rampart is at (3,2) — adjacent to that would block my patch. I can move to (2,1) or (2,2) to approach from the side. Moving to (2,2) puts me at dist 1 from Warden at (3,3) — wait, dist=(|3-2|+|3-2|)=2, not adjacent. Let me check: adjacent means dist=1. From (3,1) to (3,3) is dist=2. From (2,2) to (3,3) is dist=2. I need to be at distance 1 from Warden: positions like (2,3),(4,3),(3,4),(3,2). (3,2) is Rampart's position. (2,3) and (4,3) are not in my valid moves (max mv=3 from (3,0)). Actually wait — my speed is 2 this turn (spd:2 shown). Valid moves given are mv:3 tiles. Let me check (2,2): dist from Warden at (3,3) = |3-2|+|3-2|=2. Still not adjacent. I cannot reach Warden this turn to heal. Best plan: move toward Warden, stay away from Rampart. Move to (2,2) to approach safely, then wait.

I'm at 1/6 HP and fortified. Rampart is adjacent at (3,2). Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. At 1 HP I need to stay fortified (can't move). I'll shield_wall facing S to protect against Rampart, but Rampart is to my S so shield_wall N won't help allies from Rampart. Better to attack Rampart since it's adjacent, and use shield_wall facing S to reduce damage from Rampart. Actually shield_wall protects adjacent allies from a direction - Grid is at (3,4) which is N of me. Rampart is S of me. I should attack Rampart (dealing 1 dmg) and shield_wall facing S to block damage to any ally from south. But if I move I lose fortify. Stay put: attack Rampart, then shield_wall S to protect Doc at (2,2).

Warden is adjacent and critical — attacking will likely finish them. I'm fortified so I should not move. Doc is also adjacent but healthy; priority is eliminating the critical Warden first. After attacking Warden, use shield_wall facing North where enemies are concentrated (Grid at N, Doc at W-ish). Actually Doc is at (2,2) which is W, Warden at (3,3) is N, Grid at (3,4) is N. Most enemies to the North. Stay put to keep Fortify active.

Remedy is adjacent at (2,4) - pulse hits both Remedy (dist=1) and potentially Rampart (dist=2, just outside pulse range of 1). Pulse would hit Remedy for 1dmg. I'm standing on a trap at (3,4) - need to move off it or use it. Rampart is wounded at dist=2, Remedy is wounded at dist=1. Best play: pulse to hit Remedy (wounded, finish them off or weaken), then move to safety. Pulse hits all units within 1 tile - Remedy at dist=1 qualifies. Rampart at dist=2 does not. After pulse, move to (4,4) to reposition away from my own trap.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Rampart is wounded at (3,2) dist=3, not adjacent. Grid(vector) at (4,4) is dist=2 — must not move adjacent to (4,4). I'm at 2/5 HP, very low. I should move toward Rampart to support, but avoid Grid. Moving to (3,3) gets me closer to Rampart (dist=1) and away from Grid. Then I can use overclock on Rampart since patch is unavailable.

Grid is wounded at 5/7 HP but is 4 tiles away — out of reach this turn. Rampart is adjacent at dist=1. I have 2 heals left and Grid needs healing. I should move toward Grid to heal next turn. Moving north/east to close distance. No adjacent allies to patch this turn. Move toward Grid, then wait.

I'm at (3,2) with 2HP, fortified. Doc is at (3,4), distance 2 — moving to (3,3) puts me adjacent to Doc. Remedy is at (3,3) so I need to move to (3,4) to reach Doc, but that's Doc's position. Moving to (3,3) gets me adjacent to Doc at (3,4). I have overclocked so 2 ability uses. Move north to (3,3) to get adjacent to Doc, then attack. Second ability: shield_wall facing N to protect against Doc/Grid.

Remedy is wounded and in range at (3,3). Rampart is at (3,2). They're likely to move north or stay near each other. I should place a trap on a tile they'll pass through. (3,4) already has a trap. Placing at (4,3) covers movement toward me, or (4,2) covers Rampart's approach path. Actually, moving to (5,4) keeps distance while I place a trap at (4,3) to catch enemies moving east. But Remedy is right at (3,3) - I can attack her directly for 1dmg. Better to use pulse if I move adjacent, but at dist=2 I can't pulse. I'll attack Remedy since she's wounded and in range, then place a trap on (4,2) to block Rampart's advance path.

I'm at 2/5 HP, adjacent to enemy Grid (vector with denial) and Doc (enemy medic). Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Rampart is wounded at 2/6 HP adjacent to me at (3,2). I cannot use overclock either because Grid's denial zone blocks abilities. I should move away from Grid's denial zone to enable abilities, then use overclock on Rampart to help them. Moving to (2,2) gets me adjacent to Rampart at (3,2) and away from Grid at (4,3). From (2,2), I'm not adjacent to Grid, so overclock would be unblocked. Then I can overclock Rampart.

Grid is wounded at (4,3) with 5/7 HP and needs healing. I'm at (3,4) with a trap here. I need to move adjacent to Grid to patch them. Moving to (4,4) puts me adjacent to Grid at (4,3). Then I can patch Grid. This keeps my frontliner alive per orders.

Rampart is critical at (3,2) and within range 2. I can attack Rampart directly. After attacking, I should place a trap on (3,3) to block Rampart's movement north or Remedy's movement east. Remedy is at (2,2) and wounded - placing trap on (3,3) cuts off likely paths. Attack Rampart first to potentially finish them, then trap (3,3).

At 1HP I'm critically low. Fortify gives 50% damage reduction if I don't move, but orders say advance toward nearest enemy. Grid is at (4,3) dist=2, Doc at (4,4) dist=3. If I move to (4,2) I'd be adjacent to Grid which is in denial range — avoid that. Move to (3,3) to advance toward enemies without getting adjacent to Grid. With OVERCLOCKED I get 2 ability uses. After moving, use shield_wall facing E (toward the enemies at x=4) to protect Remedy at (2,2) — though she's not adjacent, I can at least position. Actually shield_wall protects adjacent allies from a direction. Remedy is at (2,2), not adjacent to me at (3,3). No adjacent allies to protect. Use shield_wall facing E anyway as a defensive measure for myself, then wait or use second ability. Actually with no adjacent allies, shield_wall won't help. I have no adjacent enemies to attack. Best: advance to (3,3), then advance again to (4,3)? No, (4,3) is Grid's position. Move to (3,3) first action, then (4,2) would be adjacent to Grid — avoid. Stay at (3,3) and use shield_wall E with second ability use.

I'm at 2/5 HP and patch is unavailable (0 uses left). No allies are wounded to heal anyway. Grid (vector) is 3 tiles away at (4,3) - I need to stay away from adjacency to avoid ability denial. I should move toward Doc at (4,4) to potentially use overclock on my damage dealer, but must stay clear of Grid. Moving to (3,3) keeps me 1 tile from Grid - too close. Moving to (3,4) would be adjacent to Grid at (4,3) - blocked. Best move: go to (2,3) or (3,3). Actually (3,3) is dist 1 from Grid(4,3) - adjacent, bad. Let me check: (3,4) is adjacent to (4,4)=Doc and adjacent to (4,3)=Grid - risky. I'll move to (2,4) to reposition closer to Doc while staying safe from Grid. From (2,4), Doc is at dist 3. Then use overclock on Doc if adjacent isn't possible yet. Actually I can't reach Doc this turn. I'll move to (3,3) - wait that's adjacent to Grid. Move to (2,4) seems safest - keeps distance from Grid while moving toward Doc.

Grid is wounded at 6/7 HP and adjacent to me at (4,3). I should patch Grid to keep my frontliner alive. After healing, I can wait or move. Grid is between me and enemies, so staying put maintains cover.

Remedy is at (2,4) wounded and moving toward us. There's already a trap at (3,4). I should move toward Remedy to get in range, then place a trap on a likely path. Moving to (3,3) closes distance. From there I could place a trap at (2,3) or (3,3) area. Actually, moving to (3,3) puts me adjacent to existing trap at (3,4). Let me move to (3,3) and place a trap at (2,3) to cover Remedy's likely movement path southward.

I'm at 2HP/5HP, critically wounded. Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). No allies to heal anyway. Grid(vector) is at (3,3) - I must avoid moving adjacent to it or my abilities get blocked. Doc is at (4,4). I have no patch uses left so I can't heal myself. Best option is to use overclock on an ally if possible, but both allies are enemies here. Wait - Doc and Grid are enemies. I'll reposition away from danger, moving west to (0,4) to stay safe at low HP.

No heals left, Grid is at full HP so no healing needed. Remedy is the enemy medic at dist=4. I should move toward Grid to stay adjacent for overclock support, while avoiding the trap at (3,4). Grid is at (3,3), so moving to (4,3) keeps me adjacent and avoids traps.

I'm overclocked with 2 ability uses. Remedy is at (0,4) moving west/away - dist 4, out of range. I have traps at (3,4) and (2,3) already placed. With overclocked I get 2 trap placements - place traps at key approach tiles (2,4) and (1,4) to zone the area Remedy would traverse.

No heals remaining, Grid is wounded at dist=3 but I can't patch. No enemies in range. I should move toward Grid to potentially overclock next turn, while avoiding traps at (3,4), (2,3), (2,4). Moving to (3,3) gets me closer to Grid at (1,3) without hitting traps.

I'm at (0,4) with 2/5 HP and no heals left. Grid(vector) is at (1,3), 2 tiles away — I must not move adjacent or my abilities get blocked. Doc(medic) is at (3,3), dist=4. Orders say stay adjacent to most threatened ally and use overclock on damage dealer if all healthy. Both enemies appear healthy. I need to reposition toward Doc while staying safe from Grid. Remedy is at (3,4) critical and distance 3. I have traps at (2,3) and (2,4) already placed. Remedy will likely move west toward me or stay put.
